name: Chengdu
intro: Chengdu is a strong city for travelers who want food culture, a slower urban pace, panda-related attractions, and an easy gateway to Sichuan trips.
seoTitle: Chengdu Travel Guide for First-Time Visitors
seoDescription: Plan a first trip to Chengdu with advice on where to stay, food-focused neighborhoods, and realistic pacing.
tripPace: Best when evenings stay open and the itinerary leaves room to slow down.
highlights: A strong pick for travelers who care more about atmosphere, food, and pace than checking off a huge list of landmarks.
Works well when the trip includes one signature attraction and several low-pressure neighborhood sessions.
Can become the base for wider Sichuan planning if the traveler wants to extend beyond the city.

planningPriorities.title: Keep the pace slower than in megacities
planningPriorities.description: Chengdu usually works better when you leave space for relaxed evenings and unhurried meals.

planningPriorities.title: Choose whether the trip is city-only or Sichuan-linked
planningPriorities.description: Decide early if Chengdu is the whole stop or the start of broader Sichuan travel.

planningPriorities.title: Place panda logistics before nightlife plans
planningPriorities.description: If the panda base matters, anchor that timing first and let the rest of the day remain flexible.

transportNote: Chengdu is easy to enjoy when your hotel, food neighborhoods, and one or two anchor attractions are kept within a relaxed travel radius.
